subject: Social Science (History)
chapter: The Making of the National Movement: 1870s–1947

activities_projects.title: Analyzing the Growth of Nationalism
activities_projects.objective: Understand the factors that led to the rise of nationalism in India
steps: Read the section about the growth of nationalism in the 1870s and 1880s
Identify factors such as political associations, economic criticism of British rule, and spread of education
List these factors in your notebook
Explain how each factor contributed to national consciousness
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the causes that led to the development of Indian nationalism
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of causes and clarity of understanding (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Indian National Congress Study Activity
activities_projects.objective: Examine the role of the Indian National Congress in the national movement
steps: Read about the formation of the Indian National Congress in 1885
Identify the aims and early activities of the Congress
Write a short paragraph explaining its role in representing Indian interests
Discuss your answer with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how the Indian National Congress became an important platform for political expression
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and historical accuracy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Moderates vs Extremists Debate
activities_projects.objective: Compare the political approaches of moderates and extremists
steps: Divide the class into two groups representing moderates and extremists
Each group lists the leaders and ideas of their side
Prepare arguments explaining their political methods
Conduct a short debate in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand differences in strategies used by leaders during the national movement
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of arguments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Swadeshi Movement Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of the Swadeshi Movement after the partition of Bengal
steps: Read the section about the Swadeshi Movement
Identify its objectives and major activities such as boycott of foreign goods
Prepare a chart showing how Swadeshi encouraged Indian industries
Explain the chart in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn how economic nationalism was used as a form of protest
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and correctness of the case study (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Gandhian Movements Chart
activities_projects.objective: Study major movements led by Mahatma Gandhi
steps: Read about Non-Cooperation, Civil Disobedience, and Quit India movements
Create a chart listing each movement with its year and objectives
Add key events such as the Dandi March
Present the chart in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the role of Gandhian mass movements in the freedom struggle
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and presentation of information (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Analyzing the Jallianwala Bagh Event
activities_projects.objective: Understand the impact of the Jallianwala Bagh massacre on the national movement
steps: Read about the Jallianwala Bagh incident
Write a short explanation of what happened
Discuss why it shocked the entire nation
Explain how it influenced later protests
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how historical events influenced public opinion and resistance

activities_projects.title: Mapping Important Freedom Struggle Events
activities_projects.objective: Connect important events with their geographical locations
steps: Mark places such as Amritsar, Champaran, Dandi, and Mumbai
Write the event associated with each location
Add a small note explaining the significance
Review the map with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn the geographical spread of the national movement

activities_projects.title: Newspaper Editorial Activity
activities_projects.objective: Develop critical thinking about colonial policies
steps: Imagine you are writing an editorial during British rule
Choose an issue such as the Rowlatt Act or salt tax
Write an editorial criticizing the policy
Share and discuss it with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical thinking about colonial governance
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical arguments and historical context (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Freedom Fighters Biography Study
activities_projects.objective: Learn about contributions of different freedom fighters
steps: Select one freedom fighter from the national movement
Collect information about their life and contribution
Write a short biography
Present the biography to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the diverse contributions of leaders in the freedom struggle
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reflection on Independence
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the significance of India’s independence in 1947
steps: Write a paragraph explaining why independence was important for India
Mention the role of different movements and leaders
Reflect on how the struggle united people from different regions
Discuss your ideas with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of unity and struggle in achieving independence
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of analysis and expression (10 marks)
